Empires & Puzzles Ogima Review: The Minion Destroyer

Remember when minions were a problem? Ogima remembers. This Bard brings a rhythm that doesn't just damage enemies—it punishes them for bringing friends. Discover if this specialized minion-shredder still holds up in a world of stat monsters.
Ogima: The Beat Drops (And So Do The Minions)
Ogima, The Bard of Spirits: The Minion Meta Exorcist

family, originally released during the Challenge Festival II event in late 2022. At his debut, he was the answer to the prayers of every player tired of endless minion battles. He is a specialized "anti-minion" nuker designed to punish summoners with extreme prejudice. While he lacks a costume, his base kit is so mechanically specific that he remains a unique tool in any roster. Think of him as the guy who brings an acoustic guitar to a party, but instead of "Wonderwall," he plays a chord that makes everyone's pets explode.

Ogima's design is striking and energetic. He is depicted as a tribal shaman or drummer, mid-performance, beating a large drum with intense focus. The art features warm, earthy tones with glowing magical effects rising from his instrument, symbolizing the "spirits" he commands. Unlike the polished armor of knights or the ethereal robes of elves, Ogima feels grounded and raw. It’s a great visual representation of his "Rhythm of Havoc"—he isn't casting a delicate spell; he's pounding the rhythm of war.

834 is now considered low for a damage dealer. Modern attackers often boast 1300+ base attack.
His bulk is very outdated. A modern 5-star can have nearly double his health pool (2200+ vs 1404).
. In today's meta, he cannot take a hit from a modern sniper like Devyani or Turundh without being instantly deleted. He relies entirely on his speed and unique mechanics to stay relevant.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
Ogima comes with the Bard family bonus and a unique passive that targets mana buffers.
The Bard bonus is excellent for roster flexibility. It allows Ogima to give
to any hero who is "solo" (no other family members on the team). This makes him a great teammate for a rainbow or mixed-family squad, helping average speed heroes charge just a bit faster.

At start of battle, +20% defense for 6 turns. This is arguably the best power for him right now because it helps patch his outdated defense stat, giving him a chance to survive long enough to fire.

Upon release, Ogima was widely considered "Overpowered" and a "Game Breaker." He effectively ended the dominance of the "Minion Meta" (Winter family, War of Three Kingdoms heroes). Players begged for nerfs, calling him "Skadi on steroids."

He is no longer a general-purpose defense hero. However, he is still an
in Minion Wars or against summoner-heavy defenses (e.g., Waterpipe, Queen Guowang). He has transitioned from a "must-have everywhere" to a "surgical tool."
Ogima needs to hit hard to wipe the team, but he also needs to survive.
Maximizes his damage ceiling. When he hits a minion-laden team, you want that multiplier to work off the highest base attack possible to secure a team wipe.
Leaves him incredibly squishy. He might die to slash attacks before charging.
Modernizes his survivability. Ensures he lives to fire his special at least once, which is often all you need to cripple the enemy with the mana stack.

is still recommended. Ogima's job is to wipe the board. If he fires and the enemies survive with 10% HP, they will heal up and kill him. You need that "one-shot" potential. Pair him with a taunter or protector to keep him safe.
Instantly removes all minions, bypassing their HP pools.
The mana generation stack is one of the most debilitating effects in the game.
Can punish summoners before they snowball.
His base stats are very low by modern standards. He dies easily.
Against a team with zero minions, he is just a mediocre Fast AoE hitter.
He needs the enemy to summon minions to reach his full potential.
This is his playground. He is arguably the best hero in the game for this mode.
Specifically against defenses featuring Waterpipe, Bera, Freya, or the Three Kingdoms family.
Fast AoE damage is always good here, and minions are often used as "fake healing," which he counters.
Grimble eats minions to give mana -> Ogima fires -> Alucard cleans up. It's overkill for minions.
Ludwig protects the squishy Ogima with Taunt and charges him up. Ogima's passive and family bonus help the team flow.
Even though Ogima is older, his initial hit softens the team and strips minions, allowing Anne and Jove to land their hits directly on health pools.
While Ogima hits all, if he can't kill the minions (due to mechanics preventing removal, though he usually bypasses), he suffers.
Being Fast helps, but if he gets silenced (e.g., by Bubbles) before the minions swarm, he is useless.
The biggest counter to Ogima is simply

If you pulled Ogima, celebrate! He will solve many PvE map stages where bosses summon minions (like Season 4). He will also carry you in Raids against teams you usually couldn't beat (the "Wall of Minions" teams). Prioritize leveling him, but be aware he needs protection.
Ogima is a tool, not a centerpiece. Do not put him on defense; he is too easy to snipe. On offense, keep him for Minion Wars or specific counter-picking. Is he worth a Limit Break?
is recommended to help his stats.

"Fast hero shutting down an entire team IS too much."
- Many players felt his combination of damage and mana control was broken.
Interestingly, despite the cries for nerfs, Ogima settled into a healthy niche as power creep naturally "nerfed" his stats.

Ogima is the musical equivalent of a "No Trespassing" sign for minions. While Father Time has not been kind to his defense stat, his special skill remains one of the most mechanically powerful counters in Empires & Puzzles history. Keep him in your back pocket, and when you see a minion-heavy team, unleash the rhythm.
Recommendation: A must-level for anyone who hates minions, but treat him like a fragile instrument—handle with care.
A detailed review of Ogima, the Holy 5-star hero from the Bard family. Analyze his stats, special skill, and viability in the current Empires & Puzzles meta.
